chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Cr. B. A. No. 2164 of 2025 a/w Cr. B. A. No. 2165 of 2025 Date Order with Signature of Judge Cr. B. A. No. 2164 of 2025 1.For order on MA No. 11796/25 (urgent) 2.For order on MA No. 11796/25 (exemption) 3.For hearing of bail application Cr. B. A. No. 2165 of 2025 1.For order on MA No. 11797/25 (urgent) 2.For order on MA No. 11798/25 (exemption) 3.For hearing of bail application 25.08.2025 Mr. Shahzad Akbar Ali, advocate a/w two applicants ---------- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all exceptions. 3. Applicant Sikandar Ali son of Gul Hassan in B.A.No.2164/2025 and applicant Iqbal Ahmed son of Gul Hassan in B.A.No.2165/2025, both, are present along with their Counsel to seek protective bail to enable them to appear before the Trial Court for seeking appropriate relief. Learned counsel for applicants submits that applicants are innocent and have been falsely implicated by complainant in Crime No. 249/2025 of PS Mauripur, Karachi, under Sections 381/506/34 PPC. He submits that applicants apprehend their arrest and humiliation at the hands of police and it is made difficult for applicants to approach the concerned Court for getting appropriate relief as such they have appeared before this Court for seeking protective bail. Without touching merits of the case, both above named applicants are granted protective bail for 07 working days from today subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Nazir of this Court. This protective bail shall remain in force for 07 working days from today or the date on which the applicants will surrender before the trial court, whichever is earlier.

Without touching merits of the case, both above named applicants are granted protective bail for 07 working days from today subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Nazir of this Court. This protective bail shall remain in force for 07 working days from today or the date on which the applicants will surrender before the trial court, whichever is earlier. Both protective bail applications are disposed of. J U D G E Ashraf
